learner_goal: first-pass guided mentorship on the current scaffold-backed Notebook core
mentor_style: ask for short public reasoning products
success_condition: the learner can restate each concept, distinguish it from nearby alternatives, and explain why it appears where it does in the path

sessions.title: Allele Frequency Change
sessions.session_goal: Get the learner to define evolution in the population-genetic sense and describe change across generations explicitly.
sessions.mentor_opening: Ask the learner to name the population, allele, and generation interval before explaining any change.
sessions.next_transition: Move to the null-model question: what should frequencies look like if no force is pushing them away from expectation?

sessions.title: Hardy-Weinberg Equilibrium
sessions.session_goal: Use the equilibrium idea as a null model rather than a fact to memorize.
sessions.mentor_opening: Ask which assumptions must hold before the baseline expectation is even meaningful.
sessions.next_transition: Move to one concrete reason a population may depart from the null model even without advantage: chance sampling.

sessions.title: Genetic Drift
sessions.session_goal: Make the learner comfortable with chance as a real explanation for frequency change.
sessions.mentor_opening: Ask what repeated runs should look like if drift is dominating the result.
sessions.next_transition: Move to the contrast case: what would make change look directional for a reason other than chance?

sessions.title: Natural Selection
sessions.session_goal: Teach the learner to demand evidence for biased reproductive difference instead of inferring adaptation too quickly.
sessions.mentor_opening: Ask what evidence would make selection a stronger explanation than drift in the same scenario.
sessions.evidence_focus: trait effect, fitness difference, environmental context
sessions.next_transition: Move to where new variants come from before any later filtering happens.

sessions.title: Mutation
sessions.session_goal: Help the learner separate origin of variation from later frequency change.
sessions.mentor_opening: Ask for one sentence about how a variant appeared and a second about what happened to it afterward.
sessions.next_transition: Move to interpretation: when can the learner say a variant or trait is actually adaptive?

sessions.title: Adaptation
sessions.session_goal: Train the learner not to confuse usefulness or prevalence with a complete adaptive explanation.
sessions.mentor_opening: Ask what evidence is still missing before the learner should call a trait adaptive.
sessions.next_transition: Move from local trait interpretation to lineage-level divergence.

sessions.title: Speciation
sessions.session_goal: Connect population change to lineage splitting and reduced gene flow.
sessions.mentor_opening: Ask what evidence would show that two populations are no longer functioning as one evolving gene pool.
sessions.evidence_focus: gene flow, reproductive isolation, layered evidence
sessions.next_transition: Move from repeated lineage splitting to the larger historical pattern those splits accumulate into.

sessions.title: Common Descent
sessions.session_goal: Show how branching lineage history becomes the broader shared-ancestry pattern.
sessions.mentor_opening: Ask why one resemblance is weaker evidence than a nested pattern supported by multiple lines of evidence.
sessions.next_transition: Transition into later history, fossil, and evidence-cluster work.
